label: Kernels & implicit feature maps

node.label: Kernels & implicit feature maps
node.definition: Similarity functions represent high-dimensional features without explicitly constructing them.
node.why_it_matters: The role of Kernels & implicit feature maps is to connect Linear algebra, Probability theory to downstream capabilities including Support vector & kernel machines, Gaussian processes.
examples: substrate for Support vector & kernel machines
limitations: Its benefit depends on implementation, scale, data, and hardware; the same mechanism can add complexity without improving the target workload. Its direct dependencies include Linear algebra, Probability theory.

dependencies.rationale: Kernels & implicit feature maps uses Linear algebra as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
Kernels & implicit feature maps uses Probability theory as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
Kernels & implicit feature maps uses Optimization theory as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
